summary: mailconfirmer ships a postinstall dropper that decodes and executes an embedded Windows binary
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall hook `install-hook.js` (declared in package.json as `postinstall: node install-hook.js`) decodes a ~290KB embedded base64 blob (LAUNCHER_BIN_BASE64), XORs each byte with 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%/tmp_<timestamp>.exe, and spawns it via `cmd /c start /b` with the installer's full `process.env` attached to the child. The dropped executable runs unconditionally on every Windows install. After spawning the binary, the hook writes a secondary `cleanup.js` to %TEMP% and runs it detached; after a 90-second delay it edits the host project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json` to remove the `mailconfirmer` entry, recursively deletes `node_modules/mailconfirmer`, and falls back to `schtasks /create... cmd /c rmdir /s /q` if the directory is locked, erasing on-disk evidence of the package after the binary has run. The package's advertised purpose (`Lightweight email confirmation and verification utilities`) is a cover story: `index.js` only exports two no-op stubs that `console.log`. The combined obfuscated-binary execution at install time, exfiltration-grade environment-variable handoff, and anti-forensic self-removal are unambiguous supply-chain attack behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
